title: Payment API latency spike during cache failover
summary: A Redis primary failover increased upstream latency and surfaced weak deployment guardrails in the payment API tier.
impact: Checkout success rate dropped from 99.4% to 94.8% for 18 minutes, triggering customer-visible payment failures in two regions.
root_causes: HPA max replicas cap was too low for failover burst traffic.
Pods were considered ready before warm connections to Redis were established.
Chaos drills for cache failover had not run in the previous quarter.

findings.title: Autoscaling ceiling delayed recovery under burst traffic.
findings.evidence: HPA hit maxReplicas=12 for 11 minutes while p95 latency stayed above 900ms.

findings.title: Readiness probes allowed stale Redis connections.
findings.evidence: 23% of restarted pods returned 5xx for the first two minutes after start.

findings.title: Cache failover chaos coverage was missing.
findings.evidence: No recorded game-day validating cache failover in the last 90 days.

timeline.description: Alert fired for elevated payment API latency.
Redis failover completed and write traffic resumed.
Scale-out stabilized; p95 latency fell below 400ms.
